sentence: Subjective:

The patient, a 34-year-old female, presents with a history of chronic migraines and hypertension, reporting abdominal pain that started 10 days ago with mild discomfort on the right side, worsening over the last couple of days.
The pain is described as a 4-5/10 in severity, waxing and waning, and is worse after meals.
The patient reports nausea, heartburn, and tenderness to palpation in the right upper quadrant, but no vomiting, diarrhea, or blood in urine or stool.
The patient also reports fatigue and decreased appetite.
Past Medical History:
The patient has a history of chronic migraines and hypertension, for which she is taking lisinopril 20mg daily.
Review of Systems:
The patient reports no fever, but some chills, no urinary symptoms, and no change in bowel movements.
Current Medications:
Excedrin for migraines as needed, Sumatriptan for breakthrough migraine pain as needed, Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital Signs:
Blood pressure: elevated (not quantified)
Physical Exam:
Abdominal exam: mild to moderate tenderness in the right upper quadrant, equivocal Murphy's sign
Heart exam: grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur (unchanged from prior exam)
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has acute abdominal pain with right upper quadrant tenderness, suspected gallstones or gallbladder infection; chronic migraines; and hypertension.
Problem list: Acute abdominal pain with right upper quadrant tenderness; suspected gallstones or gallbladder infection; chronic migraines; hypertension.
Plan:
I will order a CBC, chem 12, urinalysis, pregnancy test, and right upper quadrant ultrasound to investigate the cause of the abdominal pain.
I will also write a refill prescription for Sumatriptan for the patient's migraines and a refill prescription for Lisinopril for hypertension.
I will instruct the patient to avoid eating until further notice in case surgery is required.
Follow-up:
I will follow up on the patient's results today and see her back in about 4 weeks or so.
